summary.intent: Fix a clippy lint error (manual_div_ceil) in the Telegram crate, then investigate and fix a recurring install script bug where .deb package filenames had a spurious '-1' revision suffix causing installation failures (GitHub issue #496). Add tests to prevent regression. Then cut a new public release.
summary.outcome: All three goals accomplished: clippy fix committed and validated, .deb filename bug fixed in both install.sh and website/install.sh with 13-check regression test added to CI and local-validate.sh, release 20260328.01 tagged and pushed, and .claude/worktrees/ added to .gitignore.

repo: cargo-deb with --deb-version produces filenames WITHOUT a revision suffix (e.g. moltis_20260327.05_arm64.deb), while cargo-generate-rpm and the Arch packaging DO include '-1' revision (moltis-VERSION-1.ARCH.rpm and moltis-VERSION-1-ARCH.pkg.tar.zst).
install.sh and website/install.sh are kept in sync and must be identical — a script check-website-install-sync.sh already enforced this in CI.
The release workflow uses date-based versioning (YYYYMMDD.NN format, e.g. 20260328.01) and a prepare-release.sh script auto-computes the next version.
Architecture mappings in install.sh: x86_64→amd64, aarch64→arm64 for .deb; different mappings apply for .rpm and .pkg.tar.zst.
Previous fix (#493) corrected the tag URL 'v' prefix but missed the '-1' suffix bug, showing that partial fixes on install scripts can leave related bugs.
.claude/worktrees/ directory is used locally but should not be tracked in git.

code.finding: Manual div_ceil pattern (MAX_INLINE_DOCUMENT_BYTES + 2) / 3 replaced with MAX_INLINE_DOCUMENT_BYTES.div_ceil(3) to satisfy clippy::manual_div_ceil lint.
deb_file construction had spurious '-1' revision suffix. Was: moltis_${version}-1_${deb_arch}.deb, should be: moltis_${version}_${deb_arch}.deb to match cargo-deb output.
Same '-1' suffix bug as install.sh — both files are kept in sync and both needed the same fix.

workflow: macOS grep does not support -P (PCRE), so test scripts must use POSIX ERE (-E) or avoid extended regex features for cross-platform compatibility.
E2E tests (clearChatAndWait, shiki highlighter) are flaky due to timing — local-validate.sh may require multiple retries to get a clean pass; this is known and unrelated to code changes.
The local-validate.sh uses parallel fast checks; new lint/validation scripts should be added alongside fmt/biome/i18n/zizmor checks.
Release tagging triggers CI to build and publish all package types automatically via the release workflow.

friction: macOS grep lacks PCRE support (-P flag), requiring a rewrite of the new test script from a grep-based approach to a simpler pattern after the first version failed.
First attempt at check-install-package-names.sh used source/intercept approach that was fragile across platforms — had to pivot to direct grep-based pattern validation.
E2E tests are flaky (clearChatAndWait timeout, shiki highlighter timeout), requiring 3 runs of local-validate.sh before getting a clean pass.
Push required a rebase due to remote changes between the commit and push steps.
open_items: Monitor the release workflow run for 20260328.01 to confirm all package builds (deb, rpm, arch, binary tarballs, macOS app) succeed with the corrected .deb filename.
Consider whether the shiki highlighter timeout and clearChatAndWait E2E flakiness should be investigated and fixed to improve CI reliability.
GitHub issue #496 was not explicitly closed in the transcript — should be closed referencing the fix commit.
